title: Odd COPY usage not supported
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

I noticed that some Dockerfiles with odd COPY behavior that were accepted by docker build do not work with buildah bud.  In particular, when COPY --from is used to copy files from the same image that a stage is based on, buildah errors and indicates that the stage has not been built.

This is easily worked around using "RUN cp".  An error indicating as much would be nice.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Create a Dockerfile that tries to copy files from the stage it is based on:
```
$ cat Dockerfile.stage_reference_error
FROM alpine as stage1
RUN touch /stage1
FROM stage1 as stage2
COPY --from=stage1 /stage1 /stage2
```

**Describe the results you received:**
When attempting to build the above Dockerfile using "buildah bud", the following error occurs:
```
$ BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless buildah bud -f Dockerfile.stage_reference_error -t stage-ref-error .
STEP 1: FROM alpine AS stage1
STEP 2: RUN touch /stage1
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 03901b4a2ea8 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 2c7a278e2e4e done
Copying config b493569da4 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
b493569da4c25d2a7300d0d336f02d43878518732851b4c19c8ec7c02f605ef6
STEP 3: FROM b493569da4c25d2a7300d0d336f02d43878518732851b4c19c8ec7c02f605ef6 AS stage2
STEP 4: COPY --from=stage1 /stage1 /stage2
error building at STEP "COPY --from=stage1 /stage1 /stage2": the stage "stage1" has not been built
ERRO exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected the build to complete, since it did with docker build.  I can appreciate that there's not really a good reason to COPY from the stage the image is based on, so I don't feel very strongly about buildah supporting this usage.  However, a better error message would be helpful, since the stage does actually exist.

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
$ buildah version
Version:         1.12.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.10.4
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.1-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.1
Git Commit:      13330a45
Built:           Thu Oct 10 13:04:00 2019
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
$ cat /etc/*release
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=18.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=bionic
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="18.04.3 LTS (Bionic Beaver)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS"
VERSION_ID="18.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=bionic
UBUNTU_CODENAME=bionic
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
$ uname -a
Linux 01274e57e7a3 4.4.0-151-generic #178-Ubuntu SMP Tue Jun 11 08:30:22 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**
Not sure this applies, since using BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless, and vfs.  But for completeness:
```
$ cat /etc/containers/storage.conf
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body:
I can replica this difference.
Does this comment in buildah describe this situation?
https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/0f7148bae580383257353468dd8dd435935dba06/imagebuildah/executor.go#L410-L414

@QiWang19 : Indeed it does:

```
ARG MY_UI_BASE_IMAGE
FROM node:13-alpine
FROM ${MY_UI_BASE_IMAGE} as my-ui-base
COPY --from=my-ui-base /bin/sh /bin/sh # FAILS
```

```
docker build -f Dockerfile --build-arg MY_UI_BASE_IMAGE=busybox:latest .
```

works,

```
buildah bud -f Dockerfile --isolation=chroot --format=docker --build-arg MY_UI_BASE_IMAGE=busybox:latest .
```

fails.  
  
I'm working on a patch

`bud` for dockerfile success. The current master branch has included a patch for this issue.
```
$ cat Dockerfile.stage_reference_error
FROM alpine as stage1
RUN touch /stage1
FROM stage1 as stage2
COPY --from=stage1 /stage1 /stage2
```

```
$ buildah version
Version:         1.15.0-dev
```
